topic: Aspects of French-speaking Society: Current Issues
subject: french

items.q: Which verbs take 'être' as their auxiliary in the passé composé?
items.correct: Movement/state-change verbs (DR & MRS VAN DER TRAMP) and all reflexive verbs.
items.why: Students forget reflexives. Both groups use être: aller/venir/monter, etc. AND every reflexive verb (je me suis lavé). With être, the past participle agrees with the subject.

items.q: What is the 'vous' form of 'être' in the present tense?
items.why: Students apply -ez ending from regular verbs. Être is irregular: je suis, tu es, il est, nous sommes, vous ÊTES, ils sont. Memorisation is essential - être appears constantly.

items.q: Where does 'petit' usually go in relation to its noun?
items.misconception: After the noun like most French adjectives.
items.random_wrong: Either position with the same meaning.
items.why: Students apply 'all French adjectives follow nouns' too rigidly. BAGS adjectives (Beauty, Age, Goodness, Size) typically PRECEDE. Petit (size) and bon (goodness) come before.

items.q: Which French verb form correctly says 'I would have helped' (j'... aidé)?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: aurais. “aurai” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which conjunction always triggers the subjunctive?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: bien que. “parce que” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many subjunctive 'triggers' do you typically learn at A-Level (il faut que, je veux que, bien que, pour que, sans que, à condition que)?
items.why: The real figure is 6 triggers. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
